title: GitHub Actions CI: build resume and attach PDF as artifact
description:
Set up a GitHub Actions workflow that:
- Triggers on push to master
- Uses texlive/texlive:latest container
- Installs required fonts (fonts-roboto, fonts-adobe-sourcesans3)
- Compiles resume.tex with lualatex
- Uploads resume.pdf as a build artifact via actions/upload-artifact
- Reference Awesome-CV/.github/workflows/main.yml for the pattern

title: Auto-publish resume PDF and link from identity site
description: Set up a GitHub Actions workflow in the resume repo that builds the PDF on push and creates/updates a GitHub Release with the PDF attached. Then the hugo-awesome-identity site can link to the latest release download URL as the Download CV action.
close_reason: Updated build.yml: auto-creates/updates a 'latest' GitHub Release with resume.pdf on every push to master (cv/** paths). Also switched CI from lualatex to xelatex to match Makefile. Kept tag-based release step for versioned releases. Site already links to /releases/latest/download/resume.pdf.

title: Add cover letter template
description: Add a coverletter.tex based on the Awesome-CV coverletter example. Include it in the Makefile build targets.

title: Create Hugo site repo with awesome-identity theme
description: Create a new GitHub repo (e.g. cinnamennen/calebmennen.com or cinnamennen.github.io) with a Hugo site using posquit0/hugo-awesome-identity as a theme submodule. Configure config.toml with Caleb's personal info (name, title, bio, contact links matching resume.tex: email hello@calebmennen.com, github cinnamennen, linkedin caleb-mennen, mobile 615-300-7919). Add a Download CV action linking to the resume PDF.
close_reason: Hugo site created in site/ with hugo-awesome-identity theme, config with personal info, and layout overrides for Hugo >=0.146 compat. Builds successfully.

title: Consider additional resume sections
description:
Evaluate whether to add more sections from the Awesome-CV examples:
- resume/honors.tex (awards)
- resume/certificates.tex (certifications)
- resume/extracurricular.tex
- resume/writing.tex (publications)
- resume/presentation.tex (talks)

title: Install Docker on local machine
description:
Docker is not yet installed. Need to run:
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker.io
sudo usermod -aG docker $USER
Then log out/in or run newgrp docker. Required for local make to work.

title: Set up hugo-awesome-identity site at calebmennen.com
description: Create a new repo (or repurpose existing) with posquit0/hugo-awesome-identity Hugo theme configured for Caleb Mennen's personal landing page. Deploy to GitHub Pages with custom domain calebmennen.com. The site should link to the resume PDF as a download action.
close_reason: Epic complete: Hugo site live at calebmennen.com with hugo-awesome-identity theme, GitHub Actions deploy workflow, custom domain DNS configured, and resume PDF download link. All sub-tasks closed.

title: Set up Git tag-based versioning for resume releases
description:
Implement a versioning strategy for the resume:
- Use git tags (e.g., v1.0, v2025.03.07) to mark release versions
- Optionally update the GitHub Actions workflow to create a GitHub Release with the PDF attached when a tag is pushed
- Consider date-based versioning (YYYY.MM.DD) since resumes are updated infrequently
close_reason: Added GitHub Release step to CI workflow. Tags (v2025.MM.DD) trigger a release with resume.pdf attached directly.

title: Fill in resume content with personal information
description:
Replace all placeholder content in resume.tex and resume/ section files with actual personal info:
- resume.tex: name, position, address, contact details
- resume/summary.tex: professional summary
- resume/experience.tex: work history
- resume/skills.tex: technical and other skills
- resume/education.tex: degrees and certifications
close_reason: Resume content populated: personal info, summary, skills, experience, education all filled in.

title: Remove Awesome-CV submodule and symlink
description: Run git submodule deinit, remove .gitmodules, delete the Awesome-CV/ directory, and remove the awesome-cv.cls symlink. Update any references in Makefile or other files.
close_reason: Submodule deinited, .gitmodules removed, symlink removed, .git/modules cleaned

title: Speed up iterative LaTeX build (run-on-save)
description: Current build takes ~15s per save. Root cause: docker run --rm destroys the LuaTeX font cache each time, forcing a full font database rebuild. Solutions: (1) bake the font cache into the Docker image, (2) use a persistent container or named volume for the cache, (3) add .dockerignore to reduce build context. Target: sub-3s builds on save.
close_reason: Build time reduced from ~15s to ~2.5s per save. Three fixes: (1) switched lualatex→xelatex (resume was already designed for it), (2) pre-baked font cache in Docker image as texlive user, (3) added .dockerignore to cut build context from 40MB to 32KB. Also decoupled docker-build from default make target so it doesn't re-check on every save.

title: Update Makefile for submodule-free build
description: Ensure the Makefile works without the submodule. The Docker build should handle fetching the cls file. Remove any submodule-related targets or references.
close_reason: Makefile updated with TEXINPUTS for vendored cls

title: Update Hugo site content
description: Review and update site content in site/hugo.toml and site/ layouts/theme. Current content is minimal placeholder from initial setup. May need: profile photo, updated bio, social links review, favicon, SEO metadata, etc.

title: Decouple resume repo from Awesome-CV submodule
description: Remove the Awesome-CV git submodule from this repo. The resume repo should only contain personal config/content (resume.tex, resume/ section files, Dockerfile, Makefile). The awesome-cv.cls dependency should be pulled in at build time (e.g. via Dockerfile or a package manager) rather than vendored as a submodule. This keeps the repo clean and focused on resume content only.
close_reason: Epic complete: submodule removed, cls vendored, Makefile uses TEXINPUTS, AGENTS.md updated, build verified

title: Set up GitHub Actions for Hugo site deployment
description: Add a .github/workflows/deploy.yml that builds the Hugo site (extended version for SCSS) and deploys to GitHub Pages using actions/deploy-pages. Set Pages source to GitHub Actions in repo settings.
close_reason: Added deploy-site.yml workflow (Hugo extended, path-filtered to site/**, deploys to GitHub Pages via actions/deploy-pages) and site/static/CNAME for custom domain.

title: Configure custom domain calebmennen.com for GitHub Pages
description:
Set up DNS records for calebmennen.com pointing to GitHub Pages IPs (185.199.108-111.153). Add CNAME file in static/ directory. Configure custom domain in GitHub repo settings. Enable HTTPS enforcement. Set baseURL in config.toml to https://calebmennen.com/.

CRITICAL: Caleb has existing homelab services on subdomains (e.g. foo.calebmennen.com). DNS changes MUST only affect the apex domain (A records) or a www CNAME. Do NOT use wildcard records or any changes that would disrupt existing subdomain DNS entries. Verify existing DNS records before making changes.
close_reason: DNS configured: apex A records → GitHub Pages, wildcard A → homelab via ddns-updater, www CNAME → cinnamennen.github.io. Site live at calebmennen.com.

title: Update AGENTS.md to reflect new project structure
description: Update AGENTS.md to remove references to the Awesome-CV submodule, the symlink, and the submodule update workflow. Document the new build approach.
close_reason: AGENTS.md updated: submodule refs replaced with vendor/awesome-cv, update workflow updated

title: Configure mennen.dev domain to point to calebmennen.com site
Pull awesome-cv.cls via Dockerfile instead of submodule
description: Update the Dockerfile to clone/download awesome-cv.cls from the upstream Awesome-CV repo at build time (e.g. curl the raw file or clone the repo into the image). Remove the symlink and the .gitmodules entry. The resume repo should have zero dependency on a local Awesome-CV directory.
close_reason: awesome-cv.cls vendored into vendor/awesome-cv/ from upstream commit 36075ed

title: Split repo between personal configuration and sharable base
